data.description:
# Sweep: reconciler (_engine/rebar_reconciler/) — LATE, test-heavy (HTTP-classification sensitive)

The highest-behavior-change-risk batch — do it LAST. Many broad-excepts feed exception-type-dependent
bodies (`is_not_found(exc)`, `http_status(exc)`, `getattr(exc, "status_code", ...)` at
`apply_outbound.py:416,441`, `batch_dispatch.py:77,307`). Narrowing these changes which exceptions reach
the body and thus HTTP 404/error classification — so add characterization tests FIRST, then remediate.

## What
- Add characterization tests around `is_not_found`/`http_status` error classification before touching the
  catches (the `_engine/rebar_reconciler/_errors.py` helpers already exist).
- Bring broad-excepts in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/` to the observability floor. Body-inspecting
  catches stay broad-but-logged unless a narrow is proven safe by a test.
- Rollback/cleanup swallows (e.g. `batch_dispatch.py` "rollback must not mask original error";
  `reconcile.py:1155` `except BaseException ... raise`) stay intentionally broad with a justification —
  do not narrow.
- Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints (`reconcile.py`, `__main__.py`, etc.) to the named logger; keep any
  acli/jira data output semantics intact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Characterization tests cover `is_not_found`/`http_status` classification before remediation.
- [ ] Broad-excepts in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` meet the observability floor; body-inspecting ones are
      kept-broad-and-logged or narrowed only with a passing test.
- [ ] Rollback/cleanup swallows remain broad with a one-line justification; none silently masks an error.
- [ ] This package removed from the allowlist; `make lint` + full test suite pass.
data.title: Sweep: reconciler (_engine/rebar_reconciler) — LATE, test-heavy (HTTP-classification sensitive)
